name: Requisition Sweep
oracle_text:
Destroy target artifact or enchantment.
Salvage 3. (Look at the top three cards of your library. You may put an artifact card from among them into your hand. Put the rest on the bottom of your library in any order.)
flavor_text: "Confiscated for the glory of Feretha. You'll receive a receipt. Eventually."

validation_errors: Oracle text contains what looks like reminder text: "(Look at the top three cards of your library. You may put an artifact card from among them into your hand. Put the rest on the bottom of your library in any order.)"

art_prompt: A sweeping enforcement action in a grand chamber: white-armored officials confiscate glowing artifacts and enchanted relics from shelves and pedestals. Magical items crumble and dissolve under their touch. In the background, a desiccated corpse in royal robes sits motionless on a technological throne, neural cables trailing from an empty skull, overseeing the seizure with hollow indifference.. Stylized digital illustration, structured balanced composition, warm sandstone and ivory tones, golden-hour lighting with long shadows, post-apocalyptic science-fantasy setting.
design_notes: White common sorcery that combines artifact/enchantment removal with the set's Salvage mechanic. White has primary access to both artifact/enchantment destruction and Salvage. At {3}{W} sorcery speed, this is a fair rate — Disenchant effects at instant for {1}{W} are the baseline, so adding Salvage 3 at sorcery speed and +{2} is reasonable for common. The two effects are thematically linked: you destroy enemy tech and scavenge something useful from the ruins. Salvage 3 at common follows the X=2-3 guideline. This is the first appearance of Salvage in white, so reminder text is included.
